description: §8.3 real ORDER BY + LIMIT (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real"). topTwo() runs `SELECT player, points FROM scores ORDER BY points DESC LIMIT 2`.all(); the real Bun.SQL engine sorts by points descending and takes the top two, yielding [{player:b,points:90},{player:c,points:60}]. The stub returns all three seed rows in insert order (no ORDER BY, no LIMIT) — so the ordered+limited result passes ONLY on the real engine.
rationale: §8.3 `.all()` = Row[]. ORDER BY DESC + LIMIT is engine-side sort+truncate the stub cannot express (it returns the seed rows verbatim in insert order). Fork B loose-infer: scores(id,player,points) from the seed rows. Seed order (a=30, b=90, c=60) differs from the sorted top-two (b=90, c=60) — empirically the stub yields all 3 rows unsorted, so the assertion distinguishes real from stub.
